TextENGNew: The moment I opened the front door, the sound of the television rushed into my ears. Overlapping with what sounded like a panicked child's scream, I heard the rigid voice of a newscaster.

'At present, the Hinamizawa district remains in the throes of chaos.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_Tvann1007. Unidentified remains are being transported in a steady stream to the Hinamizawa district forestry office...'
"I'm home..."
The air inside the house felt taut, as if stretched to the breaking point.
A palpable tension seemed to seep from the television screen and permeate the house itself... I quietly closed the front door and walked down the hallway.

'Although the gas has not spread to the neighboring town of Okinomiya,@k@vS04/00/VT1B_Tvann1009. a strict security cordon remains in place around the surrounding area, and the outlook remains uncertain...'
......The announcer made a mistake. While the town is called Okinomiya, the correct designation is simply 'Okinomiya' — not 'Okinomiya Town.'
But... for a professional to miss such a small detail spoke volumes about the severity of the situation and the chaos on the ground.
......Come to think of it, the house itself felt darker than usual. Not empty, exactly — just heavy.
Was nobody home...? That couldn't be — the front door had been unlocked.
I called out, but there was no reply.
"Did they go out...?"

'Once again, here is the latest on the Hinamizawa district gas disaster.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_Tvann1011. In the early hours of June 22nd, volcanic gas erupted in the Hinamizawa district of Shishibone City...'
In the pitch-dark house, the only sound was the television.
"Honestly.@k@vS04/36/310100523. If you're going out, at least lock the door... It's not safe, you know.@k@vS04/36/310100524. This isn't Okinomiya anymore..."
'Survival prospects for all residents of the Hinamizawa district — the site of the disaster — are considered hopeless...'
When I opened the living room door, the only light in the dark room came from the solitary glow of the television.
'The trains aren't running! What's going on?!'
'They've shut down traffic too — how are we supposed to get out of town?!'
The screen showed the scene in front of Okinomiya Station. ...The station building hadn't changed at all from a year ago. But the scene itself was utterly different from when I'd lived there.

People with large bundles of belongings were spilling in and out of the station, shoving and jostling one another. ...With no sign of any trains coming, anger and desperation were plain on every face.
'Could you share how you're feeling right now?'

'Who are you people?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_civiMa1003. Did you come by helicopter?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_civiMa1004. Take me with you.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_civiMa1005. If we don't get out of here, we'll breathe in the poison gas and die!'
'Huh?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_repo1003. P-please wait——@k@vS04/00/VT1B_repo1004. ——Waaaah?!'
The reporter holding the microphone was grabbed by the collar and shoved to the ground. ...The broadcast was cut off abruptly.
'Ah, it appears the situation on the ground is extremely chaotic.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_Tvann1014. That concludes our live report from Okinomiya Station...'
"What...... is this...?"
"...This is... the curse..."
"——Hyaaaah!!"
A voice came from behind me.
"Ah... Grandma!@k@vS04/36/310100528. If you were here, you could have at least answered when I called..."
Genuinely startled, I turned around with a touch of anger in my voice. But Grandma paid me no attention — she was staring fixedly at the television in the darkened living room.

Instead of sitting on the sofa, she was slumped on the floor in a daze. Trembling, she clutched a set of prayer beads tightly in her hands, and as if compelled by some sudden remembrance, she began offering prayers toward the television.
Straining my eyes, I saw that Dad had come home from work and was sitting silently on the opposite sofa.
"...Dad, you're home too?@k@vS04/36/310100531. What are you all doing sitting here in the dark...?@k@vS04/36/310100532. If you want to watch TV, at least turn on the lights..."
"Welcome home."
In the dark room, the flickering light of the television played across Dad's grim profile.
...It might have been the first time I'd ever seen him look so frightening.
"Welcome home."

I heard footsteps descending the stairs and turned to see Mom coming down from the second floor with an armful of laundry. She went into the next room and began neatly folding the clothes and putting them away in the dresser.
"What a terrible disaster.@k@vS04/00/310600023. I was shocked, too."
"Y-yeah..."
When I turned back, Dad was flipping through the channels. Every station seemed to be running special coverage of the Hinamizawa disaster.
"Mom, on TV, Hinamizawa is..."
"Yes, I saw.@k@vS04/00/310600025. Apparently, poisonous volcanic gas erupted in the middle of the night, and everyone in the village died in their sleep."
"But why would poisonous gas just suddenly...?"

"Who knows...@k@vS04/00/310600027. The world has its share of frightening things.@k@vS04/00/310600028. They're saying the gas spread around two in the morning.@k@vS04/00/310600029. If something like that happened here, we'd all be dead too."
Mom explained it to me with the casual ease of someone discussing a pest problem — as if it were someone else's affair entirely.
Creak... clink, clink...
Meanwhile, the prayer beads in Grandma's clenched hands kept making an unsettling sound.
"Grandma..."
The sound seemed eerily fitting against the backdrop of the ominous, incomprehensible news pouring from the television, and a chill ran through me.
'...This is... the curse...'
I thought I heard Grandma repeat what she'd said before, and I shuddered.
"Hinamizawa village.@k@vS04/00/310600031. ...You know — the village where Grandma's family home was.@k@vS04/00/310600032. So many Kimiyoshi relatives lived there, too..."
Grandma had told me before that the Kimiyoshi family were people of standing in Hinamizawa village, and that one of our relatives served as the village head.
And every time our family visited Hinamizawa, we were received with such lavish hospitality it was almost unsettling — all because we bore the prestigious Kimiyoshi name.
But... those villagers would never greet us with smiles again. And going back to Hinamizawa itself was surely now——
"Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama..."
Grandma kept pressing her forehead to the floor, bowing again and again toward the television in prayer.
I couldn't bear to watch... Turning away, I saw Mom looking down at Grandma with undisguised irritation written across her face.
"Curses, Oyashiro-sama — nothing but ridiculous nonsense..."
She spat the words out. Mom could be moody at the best of times, but... today felt different somehow.
"Enough, Mother.@k@vS04/00/310600035. Please don't bring those musty old superstitions into this house."
"Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama..."
Grandma seemed completely deaf to Mom's words.
But... 'Oyashiro-sama.' That was the deity enshrined on our family altar — a god of matchmaking, wasn't it...? So why would Oyashiro-sama have anything to do with this...?
"...Just to be safe, I'll try calling our relatives in Hinamizawa and Okinomiya."
Mom threw Grandma one last withering look, then swept out of the living room.
Left alone with Grandma and her ceaseless, unsettling incantation, I suddenly felt very small. Unable to endure the oppressive atmosphere, I turned to Dad.
"Hey, Dad..."
"I'm going to... step out to the convenience store."
But Dad stood up as if even acknowledging me was too much trouble. He left the living room as if fleeing, and walked right out of the house.
Something was wrong... something was terribly wrong...
Dad, Mom, Grandma — all of them were acting completely unlike themselves. What had gotten into everyone...?
"Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama..."
As if to gouge at my raw nerves, Grandma's low, eerie voice echoed on. ...It frightened me. Desperate to break through it, I deliberately brightened my voice and spoke to her.
"...Grandma? Hey, Grandma."
"Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama..."
"Hey, Grandma...?@k@vS04/36/310100545. We were lucky, weren't we?@k@vS04/36/310100546. If we hadn't moved away from Okinomiya, we might have been caught up in this too!"
"...Lucky?"
Grandma, who had been praying with single-minded devotion, raised her head and fixed her gaze on me.
"That's not right, Natsumi.@k@vS04/00/310800037. You mustn't say things like that..."
Unlike me — who would have forced a smile in return and smoothed things over — Grandma made no such attempt.
She just stared at me with eyes like clouded marbles.
...Stop it, Grandma. What's wrong with you? Don't look at me with that terrifying face! Did I say something bad...? Huh?

Grandma's withered skin and the countless wrinkles etched into her face took on a grotesque cast in the dim light, sending a chill through my entire body. ...She looked almost like a corpse, and a primal horror surged up from the depths of my being.
Please, Grandma... Show me your usual gentle face. I'm begging you...!
Don't drag me into this terrifying world...!!
"Listen, Natsumi.@k@vS04/00/310800039. This is the curse.@k@vS04/00/310800040. Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS04/00/310800041. ......Oyashiro-sama's...... c u r s e."

Grandma said it with utter seriousness. ...Normally I could laugh it off, or at worst suppress my discomfort and nod along. But her expression was so charged with menace that even those options were beyond me.
"Oyashiro-sama is the deity from our household shrine, right?@k@vS04/36/310100548. But Grandma, that's our guardian god. Why would he curse us...?"

"Oyashiro-sama is wrathful.@k@vS04/00/310800043. Wrathful at the faithless who cast aside his blessings and forgot their devotion.@k@vS04/00/310800044. And at those like us, who abandoned Hinamizawa..."
"......Abandoned?@k@vS04/36/310100550. That doesn't make sense — because we——"
"Natsumi! That's enough!"
Before I could finish, a sharp voice cracked from the kitchen like a whip. ...When I looked, Mom was glaring at us. Sealed shut by that gaze, I closed my mouth.

Grandma sighed quietly, then fell silent at last, returning to her beads — creaking them between her fingers as she resumed her chant of 'Oyashiro-sama, Oyashiro-sama.' ...Her manner seemed to say that Mom and I were none of her concern.
"That's quite enough television for now.@k@vS04/00/310600039. Dinner is ready."
With that, Mom picked up the TV remote.
'In connection with the toxic gas incident, it is said that legends in the Hinamizawa district had foretold this disaster as the wrath of the village's guardian deity.'
Those words snagged my attention, and I whipped my head toward the screen. But Mom had already switched it off — the screen went black and the sound died.
"...Nonsense.@k@vS04/00/310600041. Toxic gas erupted from the swamp — that's all there is to it.@k@vS04/00/310600042. The media must be out of their minds, airing talk about curses."
Mom tossed the remote onto the sofa and switched on the overhead light. Then she stamped back to the kitchen, her slippers slapping angrily against the floor.
"Please, Oyashiro-sama...@k@vS04/00/310800046. Protect our family from the curse..."
I stole a glance at Grandma.
"Grandma...@k@vS04/36/310100554. Hey, I wonder what's for dinner tonight...?@k@vS04/36/310100555. Grandma...?"
...Grandma's strange behavior was putting Mom in a terrible mood.
Come on, Grandma, please stop. Stop praying, stop saying 'Oyashiro-sama.' Just... stop...
...I wanted to say that, but I couldn't. The fervent intensity of Grandma's prayers to Oyashiro-sama radiated from her entire being like a dark miasma, making it impossible to even speak to her.
"...Natsumi."
"Natsumi, listen carefully..."
At some point, Grandma had turned to face me squarely, kneeling with her knees pointed in my direction.
"Oh... but Grandma..."
I cautiously glanced toward the kitchen to check the situation.
The clink and clatter of dishes. ...If I could hear that from here, then our voices would certainly reach Mom as well.
"L-let's not.@k@vS04/36/310100559. It's dinner time, isn't it?@k@vS04/36/310100560. I'll listen later, okay?"
"Natsumi — no one from Hinamizawa can survive after incurring Oyashiro-sama's wrath."
"Grandma, I need to go help Mom..."
Clatter, clatter... The sound of dishes grew louder.
Mom was clanging the dishes on purpose — I was sure of it.
I'm sorry, Mom. ...The truth is, I don't want to hear about Oyashiro-sama either!
"...Natsumi... chan...?"
Grandma's wrinkled hand shot out and seized my wrist as I turned to leave.
Where in those thin, bony fingers had such formidable strength been hiding? Grandma gripped my wrist with a force that made me gasp.

"Everyone with even the slightest connection to Hinamizawa — none of them will be allowed to live.@k@vS04/00/310800052. ...When Oyashiro-sama's wrath is kindled, a miasma rises from the swamp and devours the entire village..."
"L-let go...!@k@vS04/36/310100566. Grandma, please... that's enough!"
My voice trembled as I struggled against her.
This was absurd...! Why did Grandma believe this fairy tale so completely?!
We were living in the modern age — the Showa era! We had televisions, refrigerators, cassette players — machines did everything! This was an era when science could disprove curses! ...Right?
Please, Grandma, let go of my hand! Grandma! Grandma! The fact that you can say all this with such a deadly serious face is what terrifies me more than anything!
"Natsumi, listen to me!@k@vS04/00/310800054. This is Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS04/00/310800055. The curse!"
"I said stop——?!"
CRASH!
The explosive sound of a plate shattering.
Mom stood frozen in the kitchen. ...At her feet, the plate that had been her favorite lay in fragments.
One look at the violence of the impact told me this was no accident — she hadn't let it slip from her hands.
...It had been a long time since I'd seen Mom this angry. And once Mom lost her temper, she became truly uncontrollable.
Anger at Grandma for clinging endlessly to her ridiculous superstitions. ......And anger at me for engaging with her.
No... more than that — Mom had smashed that precious plate deliberately, as a warning aimed at me. Because... her piercing glare was fixed squarely on me......
"I said enough..."
"...Haruko..."
Mom spat that out in a voice shaking with fury, then began resentfully picking up the shards from the floor. ......Seeing that, Grandma finally fell silent.
The only sound was the clink of dishes and chopsticks.
The dining room was saturated with an even heavier atmosphere than before.
Dad was eating his meal while hiding behind a sports newspaper he'd apparently picked up at the convenience store, his face buried in its pages.
...His inability to take charge at times like this was probably because he'd married into the family.
Grandma sat hunched over with her head bowed before the dinner Mom had served, making no move to pick up her chopsticks.
...I could barely taste my salmon meuniere or potato salad either — I was just mechanically bringing food to my mouth.
Mom was shoveling food down aggressively, converting her anger into appetite — and probably not tasting a thing.
"Mother, please eat your dinner.@k@vS04/00/310600045. I need to clear the table."
"...That's why... I was against it...@k@vS04/00/310800058. ............Leaving Hinamizawa...@k@vS04/00/310800059. I told you I didn't want to."
"...Not this again.@k@vS04/00/310600047. We discussed this thoroughly, and you agreed, didn't you?"
"Agreed...? Haruko.@k@vS04/00/310800061. You pushed ahead on your own..."
Grandma kept her head down as she pushed back with a reproachful tone. Then—

Mom slammed her rice bowl down so hard the impact sent all the tableware jumping. Seeing this, Dad raised his newspaper even higher and retreated behind it like a turtle pulling its head into its shell.

"If you hated it so much, you could have stayed in Okinomiya by yourself!@k@vS04/00/310600049. For that matter, you didn't have to follow us — you could have gone back to the family home in Hinamizawa!"
"We had to move because of Touji's promotion.@k@vS04/00/310600051. ...How many times do I have to explain this?@k@vS04/00/310600052. Enough already."
"...We brought divine punishment upon ourselves.@k@vS04/00/310800063. People of Hinamizawa must never leave Hinamizawa, no matter what..."
"You may be from Hinamizawa, Mother, but we are not!@k@vS04/00/310600054. We have nothing to do with Hinamizawa anymore!"
Grandma glared at Mom with tears threatening to spill. Then, in the same low, prayer-like voice, she spoke as if delivering a warning.
"Haruko... you'll be cursed.@k@vS04/00/310800065. Talking like that — Oyashiro-sama will curse you."
"Oyashiro-sama again.@k@vS04/00/310600056. Everyone in Okinomiya used to say it was nothing but superstition.@k@vS04/00/310600057. It's absurd..."
"That's not true.@k@vS04/00/310800067. Oyashiro-sama is real.@k@vS04/00/310800068. There are people who have seen it with their own eyes, and besides——"
"Be quiet!!"
BANG!
Mom slammed the table so hard that the dishes leapt into the air. Despite the violent noise, Grandma pressed on.

"You'll be cursed...@k@vS04/00/310800070. You will be cursed...@k@vS04/00/310800071. Both you and Natsumi...... are people of Hinamizawa, carrying Kimiyoshi blood...@k@vS04/00/310800072. Oyashiro-sama's curse will surely come for you........."
"I told you to stop!@k@vS04/00/310600060. If you feel that way, then go ahead — go back to Okinomiya or Hinamizawa right now, by yourself!"
"You're the one who insisted on coming with us rather than go to a care facility alone!"
"I never wanted to live with you in the first place!@k@vS04/00/310600063. And that shrine of yours — every time I invite friends and neighbors over, it's mortifying!"
"Frankly, every time you start in about Oyashiro-sama, it makes my skin crawl!@k@vS04/00/310600065. Stop forcing your delusions about leaving Hinamizawa bringing down some curse on all of us!"

"There are plenty of families who've moved away from Okinomiya besides us.@k@vS04/00/310600067. Are they all supposed to be cursed too?@k@vS04/00/310600068. What you're saying is completely incoherent!"
"I cannot keep indulging your incomprehensible obsessions, Mother!"
Feeling Mom had gone too far, I stepped between them.
After being battered by that harsh tirade, poor Grandma had slumped even lower, retreating into herself, muttering something over and over in an endless, inward loop.
Mom's anger showed no sign of abating, while Grandma had shut out the world entirely.
...I'd stepped in between them, but there was nothing I could do. I turned to Dad for help, but he was still hiding behind his newspaper.
My useless father... what a hopeless man. Feeling terrible for Grandma, who sat curled up and shrunken, I shot Dad one last glance before gently placing my hand on Grandma's back.
"Grandma...@k@vS04/36/310100571. Want to eat together in the other room?@k@vS04/36/310100572. It's not very comfortable here, is it?"
"Natsumi! That's enough."
Mom snapped, picked up her empty dishes, and retreated to the kitchen.
I stood rooted in place, watching her back as she disappeared.
I couldn't do anything...
All I could do was watch helplessly as the family I loved fell apart.
What should I do...? What on earth was I supposed to do...?
While I racked my brain, Grandma's voice reached me.
".........There really is a curse."
It was barely a murmur — faint and quiet. But the word carried a weight of gloom so heavy it seemed to be a curse in itself.
"Mother-in-law...?"
With Mom gone, Dad finally emerged from behind his newspaper.
...My pitiful father, who clearly had no intention of trying to hold things together. Looking at his face only made my own frustration rise.
Perhaps sharing the same sentiment, Grandma rose from her seat without so much as a glance at Dad and silently retreated to her own room. ...She'd left her dinner completely untouched.
The only ones left in the dining room... were Dad and me.
"Hey, Dad.@k@vS04/36/310100576. ...Mom and Grandma are both acting so strange, don't you think...?"
Saying this to Dad wouldn't help, I knew that... But there was no one else I could turn to.
I just wanted to share my anxiety with someone. That was all. ...So I'd never really expected anything from Dad in the first place.
But contrary to my jaded expectations, Dad turned to me with an unexpectedly warm gaze and gave me a reassuring smile.
"It's fine, Natsumi.@k@vS04/00/310700010. Yeah — I'm sure everything will be all right."
An attempt at fatherly composure that felt a beat too late. ...Nothing about his words sounded all right.
"Dad... but—"

"Grandma's just in shock over the disaster in her hometown.@k@vS04/00/310700012. And your mother — she's putting on a brave face, but she's worried about back home too. She's just anxious.@k@vS04/00/310700013. So give it time, and the wounds will heal."
"Yeah..."
"Grandma might say she wants to go back to Okinomiya for a while, but she'll forget about it soon enough."
"Yeah..."
"So don't worry, Natsumi.@k@vS04/00/310700016. It'll be okay.@k@vS04/00/310700017. Trust them both, and just be patient for now. All right?"
"Yeah..."
"If things ever truly get bad, I'll handle it.@k@vS04/00/310700019. So don't you worry about a thing — just keep living normally. Okay?"
His words weren't exactly reassuring, but... a child like me couldn't possibly do any more than Dad could. There was no way.
So... I had no choice but to believe.
...That was right. Dad was the adult here... If he said it would be fine, then surely it would be... right?
I knew those murmurings were just excuses I was making for myself, but...
"Yeah.@k@vS04/36/310100582. I believe you, Dad."
When I said that, Dad smiled with visible relief. ...But I was sure that deep down, he didn't believe it any more than I did......
And besides... would Grandma truly forget about the Hinamizawa disaster that easily......?
...I'd never seen Grandma in such a state of obsessive fervor before.
She'd always prayed at that unnerving household shrine morning and night, but... to speak the word 'curse' with that kind of deadly seriousness was beyond the pale.
It would be okay... it would all be okay.
Grandma was just too worried about our relatives — she was confused, that was all. With time, she'd go back to normal... surely.
Perhaps made uncomfortable by my silence, Dad switched the TV back on.
'This is... the curse...@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderFe1002. Oyashiro-sama's... curse...'
The unexpected words from the television jolted me.
An elderly woman was being interviewed on the street near my old town's station.
'...The curse came because I left Hinamizawa.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderFe1004. Oyashiro-sama's curse has...'
The old woman's eyes brimmed with tears as her voice gave out. Seeing that, Dad hurriedly changed the channel.
'Tomorrow's weather will be clear...'
With the sterile weather forecast as background noise, Dad started eating again. But I'd lost all appetite.
"Thanks for the meal...@k@vS04/36/310100586. I'm going to my room."
"...Yeah."
I rose from the table and left the dining room. ...As I walked down the hallway, the old woman's voice from the television lingered in my ears, refusing to fade.
"Oyashiro-sama's... curse..."
I'd heard the story in one form or another from various people. But still... a curse was a preposterous notion.
Walking down the hallway, I came to Grandma's room. The sliding door was open just a crack.
"Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama..."
Peering inside, I saw Grandma praying fervently before the household shrine.

"Please do not curse us, I beg you, do not curse us, Oyashiro-sama...@k@vS04/00/310800079. My family simply does not understand...@k@vS04/00/310800080. I beseech you — show mercy to these poor souls who cannot comprehend."
"Please quell your anger.@k@vS04/00/310800082. ...Protect my family from the curse...!@k@vS04/00/310800083. Oyashiro-sama, Oyashiro-sama, Oyashiro-sama..."
The creaking of the prayer beads pressed in again, an abrasive noise scraping at my nerves.
This was absurd...!
The moment I thought that, Grandma turned to look at me.
"——Natsumi..."
"Natsumi, you must pray too...!"
"G-Grandma... what are you——@k@vS04/36/310100591. ——Ow?!"
Grandma seized my hand with a grip too powerful to resist and dragged me into the room, pushing me forcefully toward the shrine.
"Wh-what are you doing, Grandma?!"
"Come now, you must pray properly too, Natsumi!@k@vS04/00/310800087. Pray together with Grandma, so the curse will spare you!"
"No...@k@vS04/36/310100594. Grandma, I don't want to...!"
Grandma believing wholeheartedly in Oyashiro-sama's curse, praying in a crazed frenzy — it was terrifying! Revolting!
If I prayed with her, would I become like Grandma...? No! I wanted nothing to do with that horrifying world!
And still, Grandma pulled relentlessly at my hand, dragging me toward the shrine. Her grip was like a vise — merciless, unyielding...!

"Natsumi!@k@vS04/00/310800089. We must pray together!@k@vS04/00/310800090. Oyashiro-sama, Oyashiro-sama, Oyashiro-sama...!@k@vS04/00/310800091. This child is my granddaughter, Natsumi.@k@vS04/00/310800092. Please, protect her!"
"Come, Natsumi, you must bow and pray too!@k@vS04/00/310800094. Natsumi!@k@vS04/00/310800095. Natsumi!"
"Nooooooo!"
SHOVE...!
Trying to wrench free from Grandma's grasp, I... pushed her away without thinking. With surprising helplessness, Grandma toppled and rolled across the floor.
I'm sorry. Are you all right, Grandma...?
I wanted to say it, but my throat was so parched that no words would come.
Even sprawled on the floor, Grandma kept murmuring.

"Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama...@k@vS04/00/310800097. Please forgive my pitiful family...@k@vS04/00/310800098. Have mercy on poor Natsumi...@k@vS04/00/310800099. Please... forgive them..."
I didn't know Oyashiro-sama...! I didn't need forgiveness from a god like that...!
Murmuring that, desperate to get away from Grandma, I backed away — one step, then another.
"Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama...@k@vS04/00/310800101. If you must curse someone, take this old body!@k@vS04/00/310800102. Let this old woman bear the curse alone!"
Paying me no mind, Grandma continued praying with her forehead pressed to the floor.
What...? What was this?
This person... was she really my grandmother?
No — she couldn't be! I didn't know this eerie person!
She wasn't my grandmother!!
"I... absolutely refuse..."
I somehow squeezed the words from my throat.
...Because I didn't want to believe in curses. I didn't want any part of it. Grandma and everyone like her — they were being ridiculous...!!
I pulled the door shut — sealing Grandma inside — and bolted.
I didn't want to see that face... I didn't want to breathe the same air!

......That was it. The ominous pall hanging over this house hadn't come from the news of the incomprehensible disaster in Hinamizawa, the village next to my hometown. It had come from Grandma's unhinged behavior.
Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama... Oyashiro-sama...
Grandma's low voice seemed to echo through my skull again and again... Never before had I felt such loathing toward her.
